# Dark Patterns and Choice Architecture

> **Use boundary:** This file is written for research, ethics, detection, and policy analysis. It describes mechanisms at a conceptual level so they can be studied, criticised, or detected. It is not an execution guide for manipulating platforms, audiences, investors, advertisers, or creators.


## Domain summary

Manufactured traction does not end at visibility. Interfaces can convert attention into manipulated action through false urgency, drip pricing, obstruction, forced continuity, sneaking, and confusing defaults.

## Signals commonly targeted

- checkout completion
- subscription retention
- upsell acceptance
- data disclosure
- cancellation abandonment

## Why this domain matters

This domain matters because its proxy signals are routinely treated as evidence of quality, popularity, safety, usefulness, or legitimacy. When those proxy signals are corrupted, downstream systems allocate attention and money incorrectly.

## Typical ethical boundary

The boundary is crossed when activity hides material context, makes paid activity look independent, fabricates engagement, manipulates ranking signals, or encourages audiences to infer real demand from distorted evidence.

## Examples to study

- OECD dark commercial patterns
- FTC/ICPEN/GPEN sweep of subscription sites/apps

## Source notes

- **OECD — Dark commercial patterns** (Policy / intergovernmental). Dark commercial patterns steer, deceive, coerce, or manipulate consumers into decisions not in their best interest; exploit biases to extract money, data or attention. URL: https://www.oecd.org/en/topics/sub-issues/dark-commercial-patterns.html
- **FTC/ICPEN/GPEN dark patterns sweep results** (Regulator / primary). Review of 642 subscription websites/apps found nearly 76% used at least one possible dark pattern and nearly 67% used multiple possible dark patterns. URL: https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/07/ftc-icpen-gpen-announce-results-review-use-dark-patterns-affecting-subscription-services-privacy
